title: Review Refactor tui/app.py to reduce complexity

handoff_summary.completed: Reviewed all 11 changed files. Architecture is sound: mixin pattern correctly separates action_ methods from app internals; lazy imports break potential circular dependencies; _make_services placement in tui.__init__ enables test patching; render.py helpers are correctly scoped; all public symbols remain re-exported from __init__.py.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. Public API is unchanged; downstream importers are unaffected. Conflict risk for tui/__init__.py and tui/app.py noted in bead metadata is appropriately low since the re-export surface is stable.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tui/__init__.py and tui/app.py are the most likely points of conflict for downstream beads importing from the tui package. The public API surface is unchanged — all symbols remain re-exported from the same package namespace.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin: action_ methods extracted as a mixin; duck-typed self.* calls guarded with type:ignore[attr-defined]. 2. Standalone action functions in actions.py take TuiRuntimeState directly; TuiRuntimeState delegates via lazy runtime imports to avoid circular imports. 3. _make_help_overlay_screen() factory on OrchestratorTuiApp exposes locally-scoped HelpOverlay to mixin without importing it. 4. Optional textual guard in actions.py (try/except ImportError) is correct — methods are only called from a live app. 5. _panel_badge and section helpers placed in render.py, not a new file.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tests cover: OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin action_ methods (test_tui_actions.py), full app integration including Tab/Shift+Tab focus cycling and panel maximize/restore across all three panels (test_tui_actions.py, test_tui_app.py), render helpers and telemetry badges (test_tui_render.py), TuiRuntimeState methods (test_tui_state.py), tree building and filter modes (test_tui_tree.py).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Two cosmetic-only observations (non-blocking): (1) __init__.py has two separate 'from .render import' blocks that could be merged; (2) _STATUS_ACTION_TARGETS is defined in actions.py and STATUS_ACTION_TARGETS in state.py — minor duplication with no correctness impact.

conflict_risks: tui/__init__.py and tui/app.py are the most likely points of conflict for downstream beads importing from the tui package. The public API surface is unchanged — all symbols remain re-exported from the same package namespace.

execution_history.summary: Implementation approved — tui/app.py complexity reduction via OrchestratorTuiActionsMixin is correct, complete, and well-tested with no blocking findings.
